# D3: Template Architecture Specification — st8 PRD System

**Version:** 1.0.0 — Complete
**Date:** 2026-05-13
**Status:** Draft
**Author:** Synthesis Agent
**Scope:** Defines all document templates generated by st8 across software, hardware, and business/launch domains.

## 1. Executive Summary

The st8 PRD system generates **20 distinct document types** across three domains: Software (5), Hardware (6), and Business/Launch (9). This specification defines the template architecture that governs every document st8 produces.

**Core Design Principles:**
- **Template-driven composition:** Every document is assembled from a `DocumentTemplate` that defines sections, their order, and whether they are required or optional.
- **Modular inheritance:** A single `st8-Core` base template provides shared metadata, header, and versioning sections. Domain-specific templates extend this base with specialized modules.
- **Hybrid content assembly:** Each section is populated from three sources — template structure, project data (code analysis, BOM databases, stakeholder conversations), and AI-enhanced natural language generation.
- **Cross-document consistency:** Variables such as `{{product.name}}`, `{{product.price}}`, and `{{launch.date}}` are substituted uniformly across all templates, ensuring the press release matches the margin analysis and the BOM aligns with the product specification.
- **Multi-format output:** All templates support Markdown (primary), JSON (structured data), HTML (interactive views), and DOCX/PDF (stakeholder handoffs).

**Most Important Templates:**
1. **Press Release (5.1)** — The "genesis document" per Amazon's Working Backwards method. Generated FIRST from stakeholder conversations; all other documents derive from it.
2. **Product Specification — Consumer (4.1)** / **IoT (4.3)** — The anchor hardware documents that embed BOM targets, certification matrices, and cross-domain constraint tables.
3. **Go-to-Market Plan (5.3)** — Bridges positioning to execution, consuming data from the press release, pricing strategy, and sales channel inputs.
4. **Component PRD (3.1)** — The most frequently generated software document, linking every file/module to requirements, test strategy, and implementation status.

---

## 2. Template System Architecture

### 2.1. Base Template (st8-Core)

Every st8 template inherits from `st8-Core`, which defines the universal document envelope:

| Section | Required | Description |
|---------|----------|-------------|
| `Document Metadata Header` | Yes | Version, date, status, authors, revision table (REV/WHEN/WHO/WHAT/WHY) |
| `Product Identity Block` | Yes | Product name, codename, category, target markets |
| `Stakeholder Registry` | Yes | Roles, names, departments, approval authority |
| `Document Status & Lifecycle` | Yes | Draft → Review → Approved → Deprecated; linked to `DocumentStatus` enum |
| `Change Log` | Yes | Version history with change summaries |
| `Related Documents` | Optional | Cross-references to other PRD package documents |
| `Signatures / Approval Block` | Optional | Checkbox-style signoff for governance PRDs |

**Base Template Variables:**
- `{{document.id}}` — UUID
- `{{document.version}}` — Semantic version string
- `{{document.date}}` — ISO-8601 date
- `{{document.status}}` — Draft / Review / Approved / Deprecated / Archived
- `{{product.name}}` — Human-readable product name
- `{{product.codename}}` — Internal codename
- `{{project.id}}` — Foreign key to project database
- `{{stakeholders.list}}` — Array of stakeholder objects

**Output formats for base sections:** Markdown (rendered), JSON (machine-readable metadata block).

### 2.2. Extension / Module System

Beyond `st8-Core`, templates compose **extension modules** that append domain-specific sections. A module is an ordered list of `TemplateSection` objects with `required` flags and `prompts` for AI generation.

| Module ID | Domain | Description |
|-----------|--------|-------------|
| `MOD-SW-REQ` | Software | Functional requirements, user stories, acceptance criteria |
| `MOD-SW-ARCH` | Software | Component relationships, patterns, diagrams |
| `MOD-SW-API` | Software | Endpoints, parameters, request/response examples |
| `MOD-SW-TEST` | Software | Test pyramid, coverage targets, scenarios |
| `MOD-HW-MECH` | Hardware | Dimensions, tolerances, materials, finishes, CMF |
| `MOD-HW-ELEC` | Hardware | Power architecture, RF design, EMC/EMI, pin assignments |
| `MOD-HW-BOM` | Hardware | Hierarchical BOM (EBOM/MBOM/SBOM), cost targets, AVL |
| `MOD-HW-MFG` | Hardware | Production volumes, assembly processes, test protocols, packaging |
| `MOD-HW-CERT` | Hardware | Regional certification matrices, timelines, costs |
| `MOD-HW-SAFETY` | Hardware | Physical safety, fail-safe behaviors, hardware security |
| `MOD-IOT-FW` | IoT | Firmware architecture, RTOS, memory maps, OTA, low-power strategy |
| `MOD-IOT-COMMS` | IoT | Communication protocols, MQTT topics, payload schemas |
| `MOD-BIZ-PR` | Business | Press release structure (Amazon Working Backwards) |
| `MOD-BIZ-FAQ` | Business | Internal / external FAQ with stakeholder-derived Q&A |
| `MOD-BIZ-GTM` | Business | Go-to-market strategy, channel plan, launch timeline |
| `MOD-BIZ-FIN` | Business | Margin analysis, revenue projections, pricing strategy |

A template declares its modules in `modules[]` and inherits their sections in declaration order.

### 2.3. Inheritance Model

```
st8-Core (base)
    ├── software-base (+ MOD-SW-REQ)
    │       ├── component-prd (+ MOD-SW-ARCH snippet + MOD-SW-TEST snippet)
    │       ├── api-specification (+ MOD-SW-API)
    │       ├── architecture-overview (+ MOD-SW-ARCH full)
    │       ├── user-story-collection (+ MOD-SW-REQ full)
    │       └── test-strategy (+ MOD-SW-TEST full)
    ├── hardware-base (+ MOD-HW-MECH + MOD-HW-ELEC)
    │       ├── product-spec-consumer (+ MOD-HW-BOM + MOD-HW-CERT + MOD-HW-MFG + MOD-HW-SAFETY)
    │       ├── industrial-equipment-spec (+ MOD-HW-MFG heavy + MOD-HW-CERT heavy + MOD-HW-SAFETY heavy)
    │       ├── iot-embedded-spec (+ MOD-IOT-FW + MOD-IOT-COMMS + MOD-HW-CERT)
    │       ├── bom-cost-analysis (+ MOD-HW-BOM full)
    │       ├── certification-tracker (+ MOD-HW-CERT full)
    │       └── manufacturing-process-doc (+ MOD-HW-MFG full)
    └── business-base (+ MOD-BIZ-PR + MOD-BIZ-FAQ)
            ├── press-release (+ MOD-BIZ-PR full)
            ├── internal-faq (+ MOD-BIZ-FAQ internal variant)
            ├── go-to-market-plan (+ MOD-BIZ-GTM)
            ├── sales-channel-strategy (+ MOD-BIZ-GTM channel variant)
            ├── product-positioning-doc (+ MOD-BIZ-GTM positioning variant)
            ├── messaging-framework (+ MOD-BIZ-GTM messaging variant)
            ├── external-faq (+ MOD-BIZ-FAQ external variant)
            ├── partnership-announcement (+ MOD-BIZ-PR partnership variant)
            └── financial-projections (+ MOD-BIZ-FIN)
```

**Inheritance Rules:**
1. **Section Order:** Child templates prepend domain-specific sections after `st8-Core` header blocks, then append base closing blocks (change log, signatures).
2. **Section Override:** A child may override a parent section's `defaultContent` or `required` flag by re-declaring the section ID.
3. **Conditional Inclusion:** If `required == false` and no data source feeds the section, it is omitted from the rendered document.
4. **Module Exclusion:** A child may explicitly exclude a parent module section via `excludeSections: ["section-id"]`.

### 2.4. Variable Substitution Engine

Variables are declared as `{{namespace.key}}` and resolved at render time from a hierarchical context:

**Variable Namespaces:**

| Namespace | Source | Example Variables |
|-----------|--------|-------------------|
| `product` | Stakeholder conversation synthesis | `{{product.name}}`, `{{product.price}}`, `{{product.codename}}`, `{{product.category}}` |
| `document` | Document metadata | `{{document.id}}`, `{{document.version}}`, `{{document.date}}`, `{{document.status}}` |
| `project` | Project database | `{{project.id}}`, `{{project.repo_url}}`, `{{project.tech_stack}}` |
| `stakeholder` | Stakeholder registry | `{{stakeholder.ee.name}}`, `{{stakeholder.cmo.email}}` |
| `hw` | Hardware schema cards | `{{hw.mcu}}`, `{{hw.bom_target_cost}}`, `{{hw.certifications.list}}` |
| `sw` | Software analysis | `{{sw.main_language}}`, `{{sw.component_count}}`, `{{sw.test_coverage}}` |
| `biz` | Business data | `{{biz.launch_date}}`, `{{biz.margin_target}}`, `{{biz.channels.list}}` |
| `ai` | AI-generated content | `{{ai.generated_summary}}`, `{{ai.suggested_section_title}}` |

**Substitution Rules:**
1. **Missing variables** render as `[PENDING: {{variable}}]` in draft mode; in production, they trigger an elicitation prompt.
2. **Array variables** iterate with `{{#each namespace.array}}...{{/each}}` syntax (Handlebars-style).
3. **Conditional blocks** use `{{#if namespace.key}}...{{/if}}` to include/exclude sections based on data presence.
4. **Date math:** `{{biz.launch_date | offset:"-90d"}}` calculates workback dates.

### 2.5. Output Format Strategy

| Format | Use Case | Primary For | Notes |
|--------|----------|-------------|-------|
| **Markdown** | Version control, diffs, GitHub/GitLab Pages, human editing | All documents | Primary format; every template renders to Markdown first |
| **JSON** | API consumption, structured data exchange, downstream automation | BOM, certification tracker, financial projections | Schema-validated against st8 JSON schemas |
| **HTML** | Interactive web views, embedded diagrams, rich formatting | Architecture overviews, GTM plans, press releases | Generated from Markdown via st8 renderer; supports embedded Mermaid/Graphviz |
| **DOCX** | Stakeholder handoffs, executive review, external sharing | Press releases, partnership announcements, product positioning | Generated via pandoc or library; branded templates |
| **PDF** | Immutable distribution, print-ready, signature workflows | Final approved PRD packages, certification submissions | Generated from DOCX or HTML; supports digital signatures |

**Format Selection Logic:**
- Default output for all templates: **Markdown**
- If `targetAudience == Business` and `documentType` is in Business/Launch group: auto-offer **DOCX + PDF**
- If `documentType == BOM` or `documentType == CertificationTracker`: auto-offer **JSON**
- If `includeGraphs == true`: auto-offer **HTML**

**Multi-format Pipeline:**
```
Template + Data → Markdown (canonical)
                    ├──→ JSON (structured extract)
                    ├──→ HTML (rich rendering)
                    ├──→ DOCX (branded handoff)
                    └──→ PDF (final archive)
```

## 3. Software PRD Templates

### 3.1. Component PRD (per-file / per-module)

**Purpose:** Defines requirements, behavior, and implementation status for a single file or module.

**Inheritance:** `st8-Core` → `software-base` → `component-prd`

**Section Structure:**

| # | Section | Required | Data Sources |
|---|---------|----------|--------------|
| 1 | Document Metadata Header | Yes | `document` metadata, `project` registry |
| 2 | Component Identity | Yes | File path, module name from `project` file explorer |
| 3 | Purpose & Responsibility | Yes | Code comments, docstrings, `sw` analysis (semantic analysis) |
| 4 | Dependencies & Imports | Yes | `project` dependency graph, `ConnectionGraph` |
| 5 | Interface / API Surface | Optional | Type definitions, exported functions, class signatures |
| 6 | Implementation Details | Yes | Source code analysis, AST extraction, `sw` patterns |
| 7 | State & Data Flow | Optional | Variable analysis, state machine detection |
| 8 | Error Handling Strategy | Optional | Exception patterns, error codes, retry logic |
| 9 | Performance Considerations | Optional | Benchmark data, complexity analysis |
| 10 | Test Coverage & Strategy | Optional | `sw.test_coverage`, test file mapping |
| 11 | Related Components | Optional | `ConnectionGraph` component links |
| 12 | Open Questions / TODOs | Optional | Inline TODO comments, `ai` elicitation prompts |
| 13 | Change Log | Yes | `document` version history |

**Template Variables:**
- `{{component.name}}` — Module/class name
- `{{component.file_path}}` — Relative path in repo
- `{{component.language}}` — Programming language
- `{{component.lines_of_code}}` — LOC count
- `{{component.dependencies}}` — Array of imported modules
- `{{component.exports}}` — Array of public APIs
- `{{component.test_coverage_pct}}` — Coverage percentage

**Output Format:** Markdown (primary), HTML (with embedded code snippets)

**Conditional Rules:**
- If `component.type == "UI"`, include "State & Data Flow" section automatically.
- If `component.test_coverage_pct < 60`, mark "Test Coverage" as required and flag in AI prompt.

---

### 3.2. API Specification

**Purpose:** Documents interface definitions for Tauri commands, REST endpoints, GraphQL schemas, or library exports.

**Inheritance:** `st8-Core` → `software-base` → `api-specification`

**Section Structure:**

| # | Section | Required | Data Sources |
|---|---------|----------|--------------|
| 1 | Document Metadata Header | Yes | `document` metadata |
| 2 | API Overview | Yes | `sw` analysis, stakeholder interviews |
| 3 | Authentication & Security | Yes | Code analysis of auth middleware, token handling |
| 4 | Endpoint / Command Catalog | Yes | Function signatures, route handlers, Tauri command definitions |
| 5 | Request/Response Schema | Yes | Type definitions, DTOs, Zod/JSON schemas |
| 6 | Error Codes & Handling | Yes | Exception types, HTTP status codes, error response shapes |
| 7 | Rate Limiting & Throttling | Optional | Middleware analysis, config files |
| 8 | Versioning Strategy | Optional | API version headers, deprecation policies |
| 9 | Code Examples | Optional | `ai` generated snippets in target languages |
| 10 | Changelog | Yes | `document` version history |

**Template Variables:**
- `{{api.name}}` — API name/version
- `{{api.protocol}}` — REST / GraphQL / Tauri / gRPC
- `{{api.base_url}}` — Base URL or command namespace
- `{{api.endpoints}}` — Array of endpoint objects
- `{{api.auth_type}}` — Bearer / OAuth2 / API Key / None
- `{{api.total_endpoints}}` — Count

**Output Format:** Markdown (primary), HTML (interactive try-it), JSON (OpenAPI-compatible export)

---

### 3.3. Architecture Overview

**Purpose:** Describes system structure, component relationships, design patterns, and scalability decisions.

**Inheritance:** `st8-Core` → `software-base` → `architecture-overview`

**Section Structure:**

| # | Section | Required | Data Sources |
|---|---------|----------|--------------|
| 1 | Document Metadata Header | Yes | `document` metadata |
| 2 | Executive Summary | Yes | `ai` synthesis of architecture narrative |
| 3 | System Context | Yes | `project` overview, stakeholder inputs |
| 4 | Component Diagram | Yes | `ConnectionGraph` visualization, ASCII diagrams |
| 5 | Component Responsibilities | Yes | `sw` analysis, per-component PRD summaries |
| 6 | Data Flow Diagrams | Optional | `ConnectionGraph` data-flow view |
| 7 | Design Patterns & Decisions | Yes | Code pattern detection, `ai` rationale generation |
| 8 | Technology Stack | Yes | `project.tech_stack`, package.json, Cargo.toml |
| 9 | Scalability & Performance | Optional | Performance benchmarks, load test results |
| 10 | Security Architecture | Optional | Auth flow diagrams, threat model references |
| 11 | Deployment Topology | Optional | CI/CD config, infra-as-code analysis |
| 12 | Risks & Mitigations | Optional | `ai` risk analysis from dependency graph |
| 13 | Changelog | Yes | `document` version history |

**Template Variables:**
- `{{arch.component_count}}` — Number of components
- `{{arch.primary_language}}` — Dominant language
- `{{arch.pattern_list}}` — Detected design patterns (e.g., ["Observer", "Factory"])
- `{{arch.cyclomatic_complexity_avg}}` — Average complexity
- `{{arch.critical_path}}` — Array of critical dependency chains

**Output Format:** Markdown (primary), HTML (with embedded Mermaid/Graphviz diagrams)

---

### 3.4. User Story Collection

**Purpose:** Aggregates user-facing functionality into stories mapped to components and implementation status.

**Inheritance:** `st8-Core` → `software-base` → `user-story-collection`

**Section Structure:**

| # | Section | Required | Data Sources |
|---|---------|----------|--------------|
| 1 | Document Metadata Header | Yes | `document` metadata |
| 2 | Product Vision | Yes | Stakeholder aspiration synthesis (`biz` data) |
| 3 | Persona Definitions | Yes | `ai` generated from stakeholder conversations |
| 4 | User Story List | Yes | Route analysis, UI component tree, feature flags |
| 5 | Acceptance Criteria per Story | Yes | `ai` generated from code + test mapping |
| 6 | Story-to-Component Mapping | Yes | `project` file/component linkage |
| 7 | Implementation Status Dashboard | Yes | Git history, test pass rates, feature flag states |
| 8 | UI/UX Considerations | Optional | Design system analysis, accessibility audit |
| 9 | Open Questions | Optional | Stakeholder conversation gaps |
| 10 | Changelog | Yes | `document` version history |

**Story Format:**
```markdown
### US-{{story.id}}: {{story.title}}
**As a** {{story.persona}}  
**I want** {{story.desire}}  
**So that** {{story.benefit}}

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] {{criterion.1}}
- [ ] {{criterion.2}}

**Mapped Components:** {{component.list}}  
**Status:** {{story.status}}  
**Priority:** {{story.priority}}
```

**Output Format:** Markdown (primary), JSON (Jira/linear import)

---

### 3.5. Test Strategy

**Purpose:** Defines the testing approach for a project, subsystem, or component.

**Inheritance:** `st8-Core` → `software-base` → `test-strategy`

**Section Structure:**

| # | Section | Required | Data Sources |
|---|---------|----------|--------------|
| 1 | Document Metadata Header | Yes | `document` metadata |
| 2 | Testing Philosophy & Objectives | Yes | Stakeholder input, quality goals |
| 3 | Test Pyramid Definition | Yes | Existing test file analysis, coverage metrics |
| 4 | Unit Test Strategy | Yes | Per-component test coverage (`sw.test_coverage`) |
| 5 | Integration Test Strategy | Yes | API endpoint tests, inter-component flow tests |
| 6 | E2E / UI Test Strategy | Optional | Playwright/Cypress config, route coverage |
| 7 | Performance & Load Testing | Optional | Benchmark suites, stress test plans |
| 8 | Security Testing | Optional | Penetration test scope, SAST/DAST tools |
| 9 | Test Environment & Data | Optional | CI pipeline configs, fixture strategies |
| 10 | Coverage Targets & Gates | Yes | `sw.test_coverage`, target thresholds |
| 11 | Defect Management | Optional | Issue tracker integration, severity definitions |
| 12 | Test Schedule & Milestones | Optional | Sprint/phase alignment |
| 13 | Changelog | Yes | `document` version history |

**Template Variables:**
- `{{test.current_coverage}}` — Current overall coverage %
- `{{test.target_coverage}}` — Target coverage % (default: 80%)
- `{{test.unit_count}}` — Number of unit test files
- `{{test.integration_count}}` — Number of integration tests
- `{{test.e2e_count}}` — Number of E2E tests
- `{{test.flaky_tests}}` — Array of known flaky test names

**Output Format:** Markdown (primary), JSON (CI/CD pipeline config generation)

## 4. Hardware PRD Templates

### 4.1. Product Specification (Consumer Electronics)

**Purpose:** Defines a complete consumer hardware product — from industrial design to BOM targets to certification roadmap.

**Inheritance:** `st8-Core` → `hardware-base` (+ MOD-HW-MECH, MOD-HW-ELEC) → `product-spec-consumer` (+ MOD-HW-BOM, MOD-HW-CERT, MOD-HW-MFG, MOD-HW-SAFETY)

**Section Structure:**

| # | Section | Required | Data Sources |
|---|---------|----------|--------------|
| 1 | Document Metadata Header | Yes | `document` metadata, revision table |
| 2 | Product Overview | Yes | Stakeholder conversations (`biz`), market need synthesis |
| 3 | Market & Stakeholders | Yes | `stakeholder` registry, target persona definitions |
| 4 | Commercials & Regulatory | Yes | `hw` BOM targets, `biz` pricing, `hw` certification list |
| 5 | Environment & Operating Conditions | Yes | `hw` storage/operating ranges, IP rating |
| 6 | Industrial Design & CMF | Yes | `hw` color/material/finish, brand guidelines, renderings |
| 7 | Electrical Hardware & Sensors | Yes | `hw` block diagram, sensor/actuator specs, power requirements |
| 8 | Mechanical Specifications | Yes | `hw` dimensions, tolerances, materials, assembly |
| 9 | BOM & Cost Framework | Yes | `hw` EBOM, target COGS, AVL, volume tiers |
| 10 | Durability & Reliability | Yes | `hw` lifetime, drop tests, cycle counts, environmental stress |
| 11 | Packaging & Unboxing | Optional | `hw` in-box manifest, retail requirements, SKU combinations |
| 12 | Serviceability & Support | Optional | `hw` repair process, FRU list, diagnostic capability |
| 13 | Certification Roadmap | Yes | `hw` certification matrix with timelines and costs |
| 14 | Manufacturing Overview | Yes | `hw` production volume plan, assembly sequence, test protocol |
| 15 | Safety & Physical Security | Yes | `hw` fail-safe behaviors, tamper detection, thermal limits |
| 16 | Risks & Dependencies | Yes | `ai` risk analysis, supply chain gaps |
| 17 | Changelog | Yes | `document` version history |

**Template Variables:**
- `{{hw.product_name}}` — Product name
- `{{hw.bom_target_cost}}` — Target COGS (e.g., $12)
- `{{hw.target_volume}}` — Annual volume (e.g., 1MM)
- `{{hw.msrp}}` — Retail price
- `{{hw.mcu}}` — Primary microcontroller
- `{{hw.ip_rating}}` — Ingress protection (e.g., IPX4)
- `{{hw.storage_temp}}` — Storage temperature range
- `{{hw.operating_temp}}` — Operating temperature range
- `{{hw.certifications.list}}` — Array of certification objects
- `{{hw.eol_months}}` — End-of-life timeline

**Output Format:** Markdown (primary), DOCX (supplier handoff), PDF (final specification)

**Conditional Rules:**
- If `hw.battery_powered == true`, include "Power Budget" subsection under Electrical Hardware.
- If `hw.wireless == true`, include "RF Design" subsection with antenna specs and pre-certified module references.
- If `hw.target_volume > 100K`, include "Supply Chain Risk Assessment" subsection.

---

### 4.2. Industrial Equipment Specification

**Purpose:** Defines heavy-duty or safety-critical equipment with emphasis on reliability, compliance, and total cost of ownership.

**Inheritance:** `st8-Core` → `hardware-base` (+ MOD-HW-MECH, MOD-HW-ELEC) → `industrial-equipment-spec` (+ MOD-HW-MFG heavy, MOD-HW-CERT heavy, MOD-HW-SAFETY heavy)

**Section Structure:**

| # | Section | Required | Data Sources |
|---|---------|----------|--------------|
| 1 | Document Metadata Header | Yes | `document` metadata |
| 2 | Equipment Overview & Purpose | Yes | Stakeholder input, operational need |
| 3 | Operational Requirements | Yes | Duty cycles, load capacities, efficiency ratings |
| 4 | Environmental & Ruggedization | Yes | `hw` extreme ranges, MIL-STD-810 references, IP67+ |
| 5 | Mechanical Specifications | Yes | `hw` dimensions, tolerances, structural loads |
| 6 | Electrical & Power Architecture | Yes | `hw` voltage rails, power distribution, backup power |
| 7 | Functional Safety (SIL) | Yes | `hw` safety integrity level, fail-safe architectures, E-stops |
| 8 | Maintainability & Serviceability | Yes | `hw` MTBF, MTTR, modular replacement, diagnostic systems |
| 9 | BOM & TCO Analysis | Yes | `hw` EBOM, service BOM, 10-20 year lifecycle cost |
| 10 | Manufacturing & Quality | Yes | `hw` factory acceptance tests, defect rate PPM targets |
| 11 | Certification & Compliance | Yes | `hw` UL, CSA, IEC, ISO, ATEX, SIL certification matrices |
| 12 | Installation & Integration | Optional | Foundation specs, utility connections, facility requirements |
| 13 | Training Requirements | Optional | Operator certification, maintenance training |
| 14 | Warranty & SLA | Optional | Service level agreements, field service requirements |
| 15 | Risks & Long-Lead Items | Yes | `ai` analysis, single-source components |
| 16 | Changelog | Yes | `document` version history |

**Template Variables:**
- `{{hw.mtbf_hours}}` — Mean time between failures
- `{{hw.mttr_hours}}` — Mean time to repair
- `{{hw.sil_rating}}` — SIL level (1-4)
- `{{hw.duty_cycle}}` — Operational duty cycle
- `{{hw.ruggedization_std}}` — MIL-STD-810 or equivalent
- `{{hw.tco_10yr}}` — 10-year total cost of ownership

**Output Format:** Markdown (primary), DOCX/PDF (regulatory submission)

**Conditional Rules:**
- If `hw.sil_rating >= 2`, include "Failure Mode & Effects Analysis (FMEA)" subsection.
- If `hw.atex_required == true`, include "Hazardous Area Classification" subsection.

---

### 4.3. IoT / Embedded System Specification

**Purpose:** Hybrid hardware-software specification for connected devices. Effectively three PRDs in one (hardware + firmware + cloud).

**Inheritance:** `st8-Core` → `hardware-base` (+ MOD-HW-MECH, MOD-HW-ELEC) → `iot-embedded-spec` (+ MOD-IOT-FW, MOD-IOT-COMMS, MOD-HW-CERT)

**Section Structure:**

| # | Section | Required | Data Sources |
|---|---------|----------|--------------|
| 1 | Document Metadata Header | Yes | `document` metadata |
| 2 | System Overview | Yes | Stakeholder synthesis, use cases |
| 3 | Hardware Specification | Yes | `hw` MCU, peripherals, pin assignments, memory/flash budget |
| 4 | Electrical & RF Design | Yes | `hw` power budget, antenna, pre-certified modules, EMC |
| 5 | Communication Protocol | Yes | `hw` MQTT topics, payload schemas, BLE/Zigbee/Thread specs |
| 6 | Firmware Architecture | Yes | `hw` RTOS, task allocation, state machines, OTA, secure boot |
| 7 | Security Architecture | Yes | `hw` hardware security module, key management, attestation |
| 8 | Power Management & Low-Power Strategy | Yes | `hw` deep sleep current, duty cycling, battery life model |
| 9 | Hardware-Software Constraint Matrix | Yes | `ai` generated cross-domain table (sleep ↔ connection strategy, SRAM ↔ buffer sizes) |
| 10 | BOM & Cost Framework | Yes | `hw` EBOM, target COGS, pre-certified module costs |
| 11 | Certification Roadmap | Yes | `hw` FCC/CE/SRRC/Matter/Zigbee timelines and costs |
| 12 | Manufacturing & Test | Yes | `hw` factory test sequences, HIL scenarios, calibration |
| 13 | Packaging & Logistics | Optional | `hw` retail packaging, ESD-safe shipping |
| 14 | After-Sales & Diagnostics | Optional | `hw` OTA update architecture, remote diagnostics |
| 15 | Risks & Integration Gaps | Yes | `ai` analysis of hardware→firmware→cloud traceability |
| 16 | Changelog | Yes | `document` version history |

**Template Variables:**
- `{{hw.mcu}}` — e.g., nRF52840, ESP32-S3
- `{{hw.flash_size_mb}}` — Flash capacity
- `{{hw.sram_kb}}` — SRAM capacity
- `{{hw.deep_sleep_ua}}` — Deep sleep current
- `{{hw.battery_life_months}}` — Calculated battery life
- `{{hw.ota_partition_scheme}}` — A/B or single
- `{{hw.secure_chip}}` — e.g., ATECC608A
- `{{hw.protocols}}` — Array: ["BLE Mesh", "MQTT", "Thread"]

**Output Format:** Markdown (primary), JSON (structured BOM/protocol data), HTML (with constraint matrix tables)

---

### 4.4. BOM and Cost Analysis

**Purpose:** Central living document tracking every component, cost, supplier, and lifecycle status.

**Inheritance:** `st8-Core` → `hardware-base` → `bom-cost-analysis` (+ MOD-HW-BOM full)

**Section Structure:**

| # | Section | Required | Data Sources |
|---|---------|----------|--------------|
| 1 | Document Metadata Header | Yes | `document` metadata |
| 2 | BOM Type & Scope | Yes | EBOM / MBOM / SBOM designation |
| 3 | Target Volume & Currency | Yes | `hw` volume tiers, currency |
| 4 | Component Categories | Yes | `hw` hierarchical category breakdown |
| 5 | Component Detail Table | Yes | `hw` part numbers, manufacturers, AVL, alternates |
| 6 | Cost Breakdown by Category | Yes | `hw` category cost ranges, share percentages |
| 7 | Volume Pricing Tiers | Yes | `hw` 1K / 10K / 100K / 1M pricing |
| 8 | Approved Vendor List (AVL) | Yes | `hw` supplier qualification data |
| 9 | Lifecycle & Obsolescence Tracking | Yes | `hw` EOL dates, component risk scoring |
| 10 | Cost Reduction Roadmap | Optional | VAVE activities, substitution timelines |
| 11 | Should-Cost Analysis | Optional | `ai` estimated fair cost per category |
| 12 | Cross-Reference to Certification | Optional | `hw` component-level compliance (RoHS, REACH) |
| 13 | Changelog | Yes | `document` version history |

**Template Variables:**
- `{{bom.type}}` — EBOM | MBOM | SBOM
- `{{bom.total_target_cost}}` — Sum of target costs
- `{{bom.currency}}` — CNY | USD | EUR
- `{{bom.target_volume}}` — Active volume tier
- `{{bom.category_count}}` — Number of categories
- `{{bom.component_count}}` — Total line items
- `{{bom.high_risk_components}}` — Array of single-source or EOL-near parts

**Output Format:** JSON (primary — structured data), Markdown (human-readable), XLSX/CSV (supplier exchange)

---

### 4.5. Certification Tracker

**Purpose:** Tracks every certification as a first-class project workstream with timelines, costs, dependencies, and status.

**Inheritance:** `st8-Core` → `hardware-base` → `certification-tracker` (+ MOD-HW-CERT full)

**Section Structure:**

| # | Section | Required | Data Sources |
|---|---------|----------|--------------|
| 1 | Document Metadata Header | Yes | `document` metadata |
| 2 | Target Markets | Yes | `biz` launch regions |
| 3 | Certification Matrix | Yes | `hw` certifications by market with scope |
| 4 | Timeline & Cost Summary | Yes | `hw` per-certification weeks and USD/EUR/CNY cost |
| 5 | Pre-Certified Module Strategy | Yes | `hw` module reuse plan (WiFi/BLE pre-certified) |
| 6 | Dependency Graph | Yes | `hw` cert dependencies (e.g., engineering samples → pre-test → submission) |
| 7 | Critical Path Analysis | Yes | `ai` derived from timeline + dependencies |
| 8 | Risk & Mitigation | Yes | `ai` risk of failure, contingency plans |
| 9 | Post-Market Surveillance | Optional | Factory audit schedules, certificate maintenance |
| 10 | Data Privacy & Cybersecurity Compliance | Optional | PIPL, GDPR, IoT security standards |
| 11 | Changelog | Yes | `document` version history |

**Certification Entry Format:**
```yaml
certifications:
  - name: "FCC"
    region: "North America"
    scope: "WiFi + BLE radio"
    authority: "FCC"
    timeline_weeks: [4, 6]
    cost_usd: 8000
    status: "Planned"
    dependencies: ["Engineering samples", "RF calibration"]
    critical_path: true
```

**Output Format:** JSON (primary — machine-actionable), Markdown (Gantt-style timeline), HTML (interactive dashboard)

---

### 4.6. Manufacturing Process Document

**Purpose:** Defines how the product is built, tested, packaged, and shipped at volume.

**Inheritance:** `st8-Core` → `hardware-base` → `manufacturing-process-doc` (+ MOD-HW-MFG full)

**Section Structure:**

| # | Section | Required | Data Sources |
|---|---------|----------|--------------|
| 1 | Document Metadata Header | Yes | `document` metadata |
| 2 | Production Volume Plan | Yes | `hw` EVT → DVT → PVT → MP ramp |
| 3 | Tooling & Equipment | Yes | `hw` mold costs, SMT line capabilities, test fixtures |
| 4 | Assembly Process & Work Instructions | Yes | `hw` step-by-step sequences, torque specs, curing profiles |
| 5 | PCB Fabrication Specs | Optional | Layer count, impedance control, surface finish |
| 6 | Quality Gates & Inspection | Yes | `hw` incoming QC, in-process inspection, final test |
| 7 | Factory Acceptance Test (FAT) Protocol | Yes | `hw` test items, methods, pass/fail criteria |
| 8 | Traceability & Serialization | Yes | `hw` serial number assignment, lot tracking |
| 9 | Packaging Specifications | Yes | `hw` ESD-safe packaging, moisture barrier, retail box |
| 10 | Yield Targets & Defect Rates | Optional | `hw` PPM targets, known failure modes |
| 11 | Workback Schedule | Optional | `biz` launch date → manufacturing milestones |
| 12 | Changelog | Yes | `document` version history |

**Template Variables:**
- `{{mfg.evt_units}}` — EVT unit count
- `{{mfg.dvt_units}}` — DVT unit count
- `{{mfg.pvt_units}}` — PVT unit count
- `{{mfg.mp_volume}}` — Mass production volume
- `{{mfg.mold_cost_usd}}` — Tooling investment
- `{{mfg.target_ppm}}` — Defect rate target
- `{{mfg.fat_test_count}}` — Number of FAT test items

**Output Format:** Markdown (primary), DOCX (factory handoff), PDF (quality system record)

## 5. Business / Launch Templates

### 5.1. Press Release (Amazon PR/FAQ Style)

**Purpose:** The "genesis document" per Amazon's Working Backwards. Generated FIRST from stakeholder conversations; if it isn't compelling, the product shouldn't be built.

**Inheritance:** `st8-Core` → `business-base` (+ MOD-BIZ-PR) → `press-release`

**Section Structure:**

| # | Section | Required | Data Sources |
|---|---------|----------|--------------|
| 1 | Document Metadata Header | Yes | `document` metadata |
| 2 | Headline | Yes | `product.name` + key benefit + `ai` copywriting |
| 3 | Subhead | Yes | Supporting detail, differentiator |
| 4 | Dateline | Yes | `biz.launch_city`, `biz.launch_date` |
| 5 | Lead Paragraph | Yes | Who, what, when, where, why — synthesized from stakeholder inputs |
| 6 | Body Paragraphs | Yes | Feature details, use cases, `ai` narrative |
| 7 | Quotes | Yes | `stakeholder` CEO, product lead, partner exec quotes (with attribution) |
| 8 | About the Company | Yes | Boilerplate from `biz` company profile |
| 9 | Contact Information | Yes | Media relations contact |
| 10 | Boilerplate Product Specs | Optional | 2-3 key technical specs for journalists |
| 11 | Availability & Pricing | Optional | `biz.price`, `biz.availability_date`, `biz.channels` |
| 12 | Changelog | Yes | `document` version history |

**Template Variables:**
- `{{pr.headline}}` — Compelling headline
- `{{pr.subhead}}` — One-line elaboration
- `{{pr.lead}}` — 2-3 sentence lead paragraph
- `{{pr.launch_date}}` — Specific date (not quarter)
- `{{pr.price}}` — Specific price (e.g., $1299)
- `{{pr.channels}}` — Array of launch channels
- `{{pr.quotes}}` — Array of quote objects with attribution

**Output Format:** Markdown (primary), DOCX (media handoff), PDF (embargo distribution)

**Conditional Rules:**
- If `biz.product_type == "hardware"`, include "Boilerplate Product Specs" subsection.
- If `biz.partnership == true`, include joint-quote block with partner executive.

---

### 5.2. Internal FAQ

**Purpose:** Resolves internal questions, concerns, and edge cases that surfaced during press release writing. More important than the press release for internal alignment (per Vogels).

**Inheritance:** `st8-Core` → `business-base` (+ MOD-BIZ-FAQ) → `internal-faq`

**Section Structure:**

| # | Section | Required | Data Sources |
|---|---------|----------|--------------|
| 1 | Document Metadata Header | Yes | `document` metadata |
| 2 | Product Summary | Yes | Synthesis of press release |
| 3 | Questions from Press Release Writing | Yes | `ai` generated from PR draft tensions |
| 4 | Technical Questions | Yes | Engineering stakeholder inputs |
| 5 | Business & Pricing Questions | Yes | CFO, sales director inputs |
| 6 | Competitive Questions | Yes | Market analysis, competitor feature matrix |
| 7 | Timeline & Resource Questions | Yes | PM, manufacturing inputs |
| 8 | Risk & Mitigation Questions | Yes | `ai` generated from cross-stakeholder tension |
| 9 | Open Questions (Unresolved) | Yes | Stakeholder conversation gaps |
| 10 | Changelog | Yes | `document` version history |

**FAQ Entry Format:**
```markdown
**Q: {{faq.question}}**
**A:** {{faq.answer}}

*Source: {{faq.source_stakeholder}} | Confidence: {{faq.confidence}}*
```

**Template Variables:**
- `{{faq.questions}}` — Array of FAQ objects
- `{{faq.unresolved_count}}` — Number of open questions
- `{{faq.confidence_avg}}` — Average confidence score

**Output Format:** Markdown (primary), DOCX (executive handoff), HTML (internal wiki)

---

### 5.3. Go-to-Market Plan

**Purpose:** Tactical plan detailing how the company executes a successful product release, promotion, and sale.

**Inheritance:** `st8-Core` → `business-base` (+ MOD-BIZ-GTM) → `go-to-market-plan`

**Section Structure:**

| # | Section | Required | Data Sources |
|---|---------|----------|--------------|
| 1 | Document Metadata Header | Yes | `document` metadata |
| 2 | Executive Summary | Yes | `biz` positioning, launch date, key metrics |
| 3 | Target Market & Personas | Yes | `stakeholder` interviews, market research |
| 4 | Value Proposition & Messaging | Yes | Press release, positioning document |
| 5 | Pricing Strategy | Yes | `biz` financial model, competitive analysis |
| 6 | Channel Strategy | Yes | `stakeholder` sales director inputs, partner conversations |
| 7 | Marketing Plan & Campaigns | Yes | CMO input, campaign concepts, `biz` budget |
| 8 | Sales Enablement | Yes | Training plan, collateral list, demo scripts |
| 9 | Launch Timeline (Workback) | Yes | `biz` launch date → milestone dates |
| 10 | Success Metrics & KPIs | Yes | `biz` revenue targets, adoption goals |
| 11 | Budget & Resource Allocation | Yes | `biz` marketing spend, sales incentives |
| 12 | Risk & Contingency | Optional | `ai` risk analysis |
| 13 | Changelog | Yes | `document` version history |

**Template Variables:**
- `{{gtm.launch_date}}` — Launch date
- `{{gtm.budget_usd}}` — Total launch budget
- `{{gtm.channels}}` — Array of channels with priority
- `{{gtm.personas}}` — Array of target personas
- `{{gtm.kpis}}` — Array of KPI objects (name, target, measurement)
- `{{gtm.workback_milestones}}` — Array of milestone objects

**Output Format:** Markdown (primary), DOCX/PDF (board presentation), HTML (interactive timeline)

---

### 5.4. Sales Channel Strategy

**Purpose:** Defines where and how the product is sold, including partner economics and retailer requirements.

**Inheritance:** `st8-Core` → `business-base` (+ MOD-BIZ-GTM channel variant) → `sales-channel-strategy`

**Section Structure:**

| # | Section | Required | Data Sources |
|---|---------|----------|--------------|
| 1 | Document Metadata Header | Yes | `document` metadata |
| 2 | Channel Overview | Yes | Direct, retail, online, wholesale, OEM mix |
| 3 | Channel Economics | Yes | Margins, co-op funds, logistics costs |
| 4 | Partner Profiles | Yes | Key accounts, buying groups, distributors |
| 5 | Launch Sequence | Yes | Which channels first, ramp schedule |
| 6 | Retailer Requirements | Yes | Merchandising, training, promotional support |
| 7 | Performance Metrics | Yes | Sell-in, sell-through, inventory turns |
| 8 | Sales Playbook Summary | Optional | Scripts, objection handling, competitive positioning |
| 9 | Demo Guidelines | Optional | How to demonstrate key value propositions |
| 10 | Changelog | Yes | `document` version history |

**Template Variables:**
- `{{channel.mix}}` — Array of channel objects (name, percentage, margin)
- `{{channel.primary_retailers}}` — Array of retailer names
- `{{channel.coop_fund_pct}}` — Co-op marketing fund percentage
- `{{channel.target_sellthrough}}` — Target sell-through rate

**Output Format:** Markdown (primary), DOCX (sales team handoff), PDF (partner brief)

---

### 5.5. Product Positioning Document

**Purpose:** Defines how the product lives in customers' minds — "the process of deciding and communicating how you want your market to think and feel about your product."

**Inheritance:** `st8-Core` → `business-base` (+ MOD-BIZ-GTM positioning variant) → `product-positioning-doc`

**Section Structure:**

| # | Section | Required | Data Sources |
|---|---------|----------|--------------|
| 1 | Document Metadata Header | Yes | `document` metadata |
| 2 | Target Persona | Yes | Demographics, psychographics, goals, fears |
| 3 | Competitive Landscape | Yes | Competitor analysis, market gaps |
| 4 | Unique Value Proposition | Yes | Feature differentiation + emotional benefit |
| 5 | Positioning Statement | Yes | "For [target], [product] is the [category] that [benefit]" |
| 6 | Messaging Pillars | Yes | 3-4 key themes with proof points |
| 7 | Tone & Voice Guidelines | Yes | Brand personality, language guidelines |
| 8 | Emotional Resonance Map | Optional | `ai` generated sensory language, story angles |
| 9 | Positioning Validation | Optional | Stakeholder confidence scores |
| 10 | Changelog | Yes | `document` version history |

**Template Variables:**
- `{{positioning.target_persona}}` — Primary persona name/description
- `{{positioning.statement}}` — Full positioning statement
- `{{positioning.pillars}}` — Array of messaging pillars
- `{{positioning.competitors}}` — Array of competitor objects
- `{{positioning.differentiators}}` — Array of unique claims

**Output Format:** Markdown (primary), DOCX (brand team handoff), PDF (agency brief)

---

### 5.6. Messaging Framework

**Purpose:** Operationalizes the positioning document into reusable messaging components for every channel and audience.

**Inheritance:** `st8-Core` → `business-base` (+ MOD-BIZ-GTM messaging variant) → `messaging-framework`

**Section Structure:**

| # | Section | Required | Data Sources |
|---|---------|----------|--------------|
| 1 | Document Metadata Header | Yes | `document` metadata |
| 2 | Core Narrative | Yes | 25-word, 50-word, 100-word versions |
| 3 | Audience-Specific Messaging | Yes | Per-audience adaptations (technical, business, consumer) |
| 4 | Channel-Specific Messaging | Yes | Web, email, social, PR, sales deck adaptations |
| 5 | Proof Points & Evidence | Yes | Data, testimonials, third-party validation |
| 6 | Competitive Battle Cards | Optional | Per-competitor objection/response pairs |
| 7 | Forbidden Words & Phrases | Optional | Terms to avoid per brand guidelines |
| 8 | Localization Notes | Optional | Regional adaptation guidelines |
| 9 | Changelog | Yes | `document` version history |

**Template Variables:**
- `{{messaging.narrative_25}}` — 25-word narrative
- `{{messaging.narrative_50}}` — 50-word narrative
- `{{messaging.narrative_100}}` — 100-word narrative
- `{{messaging.audiences}}` — Array of audience-specific message objects
- `{{messaging.channels}}` — Array of channel-specific message objects

**Output Format:** Markdown (primary), DOCX (marketing team handoff), JSON (CMS integration)

---

### 5.7. External FAQ

**Purpose:** Customer-facing Q&A addressing product features, compatibility, support, and purchase questions.

**Inheritance:** `st8-Core` → `business-base` (+ MOD-BIZ-FAQ external variant) → `external-faq`

**Section Structure:**

| # | Section | Required | Data Sources |
|---|---------|----------|--------------|
| 1 | Document Metadata Header | Yes | `document` metadata |
| 2 | Product Overview Questions | Yes | Synthesized from press release |
| 3 | Features & Compatibility | Yes | Technical spec summaries |
| 4 | Pricing & Availability | Yes | `biz` pricing, channels, launch date |
| 5 | Support & Warranty | Yes | `hw` warranty terms, service plan |
| 6 | Technical Specifications | Optional | Condensed from product spec |
| 7 | Comparison with Previous Version | Optional | Migration guide, upgrade path |
| 8 | Purchase & Returns | Optional | Retailer links, return policy |
| 9 | Changelog | Yes | `document` version history |

**Output Format:** Markdown (primary), HTML (website FAQ page), JSON (chatbot knowledge base)

---

### 5.8. Partnership Announcement

**Purpose:** Joint press release for technology partnerships, distribution deals, or co-development agreements.

**Inheritance:** `st8-Core` → `business-base` (+ MOD-BIZ-PR partnership variant) → `partnership-announcement`

**Section Structure:**

| # | Section | Required | Data Sources |
|---|---------|----------|--------------|
| 1 | Document Metadata Header | Yes | `document` metadata |
| 2 | Headline | Yes | Both company names + partnership nature |
| 3 | Joint Value Proposition | Yes | `stakeholder` inputs from both sides |
| 4 | Technical Integration Details | Optional | API, hardware, or platform integration summary |
| 5 | Customer Benefit Narrative | Yes | `ai` synthesized use cases |
| 6 | Quotes from Both Partners | Yes | `stakeholder` exec quotes |
| 7 | Availability & Next Steps | Yes | `biz` timeline, contact for integration |
| 8 | About Partner A | Yes | Partner boilerplate |
| 9 | About Partner B | Yes | Partner boilerplate |
| 10 | Contact Information | Yes | Both partners' media contacts |
| 11 | Changelog | Yes | `document` version history |

**Template Variables:**
- `{{partnership.partner_a_name}}` — First partner
- `{{partnership.partner_b_name}}` — Second partner
- `{{partnership.type}}` — Technology / Distribution / Co-development
- `{{partnership.quotes}}` — Array of dual-attribution quotes
- `{{partnership.next_steps}}` — Call to action

**Output Format:** Markdown (primary), DOCX (joint handoff), PDF (embargo)

---

### 5.9. Financial Projections

**Purpose:** Makes the product investable by projecting revenue, costs, margins, and stock price impact.

**Inheritance:** `st8-Core` → `business-base` (+ MOD-BIZ-FIN) → `financial-projections`

**Section Structure:**

| # | Section | Required | Data Sources |
|---|---------|----------|--------------|
| 1 | Document Metadata Header | Yes | `document` metadata |
| 2 | Executive Financial Summary | Yes | `biz` revenue, margin, and investment summary |
| 3 | Market Sizing (TAM/SAM/SOM) | Yes | Market research, stakeholder inputs |
| 4 | Pricing Strategy & SKU Architecture | Yes | `biz` price points, tiers, bundles |
| 5 | Unit Forecasts by Channel & Region | Yes | `biz` sales projections |
| 6 | Revenue Ramp Model | Yes | Launch → steady-state trajectory |
| 7 | Margin Analysis (CoGS → Gross → Net) | Yes | `hw` BOM, `biz` channel margins |
| 8 | Cost Targets & Budget | Yes | `hw` tooling, `hw` certification, `biz` marketing |
| 9 | Sensitivity Analysis | Optional | Price/volume/cost scenario modeling |
| 10 | Stock Price Impact (if public) | Optional | Analyst consensus, guidance impact |
| 11 | Break-Even Analysis | Yes | Unit breakeven, timeline to profitability |
| 12 | Risk Factors to Financial Plan | Yes | `ai` risk analysis |
| 13 | Changelog | Yes | `document` version history |

**Template Variables:**
- `{{fin.tam_usd}}` — Total addressable market
- `{{fin.sam_usd}}` — Serviceable addressable market
- `{{fin.som_usd}}` — Serviceable obtainable market
- `{{fin.y1_revenue_usd}}` — Year 1 revenue projection
- `{{fin.target_margin_pct}}` — Target gross margin
- `{{fin.breakeven_units}}` — Breakeven unit volume
- `{{fin.breakeven_months}}` — Months to breakeven
- `{{fin.cogs_target}}` — Target cost of goods sold

**Output Format:** Markdown (primary), JSON (structured financial model), XLSX (spreadsheet export), DOCX (board presentation)

## 6. Cross-Template Data Flows

The PRD package is not a collection of independent documents. It is a **network of dependencies** where each document both consumes and produces data. The following diagram shows the canonical generation order and data flows:

```
Stakeholder Conversations
         |
         v
+------------------+
|  Press Release   | <-- Generated FIRST (Amazon Working Backwards)
+------------------+
         |
    +----+----+
    |         |
    v         v
+-------+  +--------+
|  FAQ  |  | Product|
+-------+  |Position|
    |      +--------+
    |         |
    v         v
+-------+  +--------+
|GTM Plan | Sales   |
+-------+  |Channel |
    |      |Strategy|
    |      +--------+
    |         |
    v         v
+-------+  +--------+
|Margin |  |Retailer|
|Analysis| |Preso   |
+-------+  +--------+
    |         |
    v         v
+-------+  +--------+
|Workback| |Partner |
|Schedule| |Brief   |
+-------+  +--------+
    |
    v
+-------+
|Financial|
|Projections|
+-------+
```

### Key Data Flows

| Producer → Consumer | Data Transferred | Consistency Rule |
|---------------------|------------------|------------------|
| Press Release → GTM Plan | Messaging, launch date, target audience | Launch date must match |
| Press Release → Pricing Strategy | Price point anchors margin analysis | Price must be identical |
| Press Release → Product Positioning | Value proposition, differentiators | Claims must be supported |
| Product Positioning → Messaging Framework | Pillars, tone, voice | Pillars must be identical |
| Product Positioning → External FAQ | Feature claims, compatibility | Feature list must match spec |
| GTM Plan → Sales Channel Strategy | Channel mix, budget, timeline | Channels must be identical |
| GTM Plan → Workback Schedule | Milestones, launch date | Dates must be identical |
| Sales Channel Strategy → Retailer Presentation | Partner profiles, economics | Margin structure must match |
| Pricing Strategy → Margin Analysis | MSRP, target margin, CoGS | Math must reconcile |
| Margin Analysis → Financial Projections | Unit economics, revenue ramp | Forecasts must derive from margins |
| BOM & Cost Analysis → Margin Analysis | Component costs, volume pricing | CoGS must derive from BOM |
| BOM & Cost Analysis → Product Specification | BOM target, component list | Component list must match |
| Certification Tracker → Workback Schedule | Certification milestones | Timeline must include cert lead times |
| Certification Tracker → Manufacturing Process | Factory audit schedules | FAT must occur before cert submission |
| Hardware Spec → IoT/Embedded Spec | MCU, pin assignments, power budget | Constraint matrix must be consistent |
| Hardware Spec → BOM & Cost Analysis | Component list, AVL | BOM must derive from spec |
| Component PRD → Architecture Overview | Component responsibilities, interfaces | Component count must match |
| Component PRD → API Specification | Exported functions, signatures | API surface must match code |
| Component PRD → Test Strategy | Test coverage, test file mapping | Coverage numbers must match |
| User Story Collection → Component PRD | Story-to-component mapping | Stories must map to real components |
| User Story Collection → Test Strategy | Acceptance criteria | Criteria must be testable |

### Consistency Enforcement Rules

1. **Price Lock:** `{{biz.price}}` in press release = `{{biz.price}}` in GTM plan = `{{biz.price}}` in margin analysis = `{{biz.price}}` in financial projections.
2. **Launch Date Lock:** `{{biz.launch_date}}` in press release = `{{gtm.launch_date}}` = `{{cert.critical_path.end_date}}` = `{{mfg.workback.launch}}`.
3. **Feature Lock:** Features listed in press release = features in product spec = features in external FAQ = features in sales playbook.
4. **BOM Lock:** Component list in product spec = component list in BOM analysis = component list in manufacturing process.
5. **Certification Lock:** Certification list in product spec = certification tracker entries = manufacturing readiness criteria.

**Validation Trigger:** When any producer document is updated, the system flags all consumer documents for consistency review. A "PRD Package Health" dashboard shows red/yellow/green status per consistency rule.

---

## 7. Template-to-Schema Mapping

This section maps each template to the schema cards (data sources) defined in the st8 PRD system.

| Template | Primary Schema Cards | Secondary Schema Cards | AI Prompts |
|----------|---------------------|------------------------|------------|
| Component PRD | `project` (file explorer), `sw` (code analysis) | `ConnectionGraph`, `ConnectionVerifier` | Generate purpose narrative from code comments; suggest open questions from TODO patterns |
| API Specification | `sw` (type definitions, signatures) | `project` (route handlers, Tauri commands) | Generate request/response examples; infer auth flows |
| Architecture Overview | `project`, `ConnectionGraph`, `sw` | `ConnectionVerifier` | Generate pattern descriptions; create rationale for design decisions |
| User Story Collection | `project` (route/UI analysis), `biz` (personas) | `stakeholder` inputs | Map stories to components; infer acceptance criteria from code |
| Test Strategy | `sw` (test files, coverage) | `project` (CI config) | Suggest missing test scenarios; identify flaky patterns |
| Product Spec — Consumer | `hw` (BOM, CMF, environmental), `biz` (pricing, channels) | `stakeholder` (ID, ME, EE inputs) | Generate durability test recommendations; suggest certification defaults |
| Industrial Equipment Spec | `hw` (ruggedization, safety, TCO) | `stakeholder` (operators, maintenance) | Generate FMEA suggestions; create service interval recommendations |
| IoT/Embedded Spec | `hw` (MCU, RF, power), `sw` (firmware constraints) | `stakeholder` (firmware, cloud engineers) | Generate constraint matrix; suggest memory budget allocations |
| BOM & Cost Analysis | `hw` (components, AVL, pricing) | `project` (supplier APIs) | Suggest alternates; flag obsolescence risks |
| Certification Tracker | `hw` (cert list, timelines) | `biz` (target markets) | Generate dependency graph; estimate critical path |
| Manufacturing Process Doc | `hw` (assembly, test protocols) | `biz` (launch schedule) | Generate work instructions; suggest quality gates |
| Press Release | `biz` (positioning, pricing, launch), `stakeholder` (quotes) | `product` (features, specs) | Write headline options; generate benefit-oriented body copy |
| Internal FAQ | `biz`, `stakeholder` (all departments) | `product`, `hw`, `sw` | Generate questions from cross-stakeholder tensions; answer from available data |
| Go-to-Market Plan | `biz` (budget, channels, timeline), `stakeholder` (CMO, sales) | `product` (features, pricing) | Generate campaign concepts; build workback schedule |
| Sales Channel Strategy | `biz` (channel economics), `stakeholder` (sales director) | `product` (retail requirements) | Generate pitch angles; create objection handling |
| Product Positioning Doc | `biz` (competitive analysis), `stakeholder` (PM, marketing) | `product` (differentiators) | Generate positioning statement variants; create emotional resonance map |
| Messaging Framework | `biz` (positioning, tone) | `product` (features) | Generate audience-specific adaptations; create channel copy |
| External FAQ | `product` (specs, features), `biz` (pricing, support) | `hw` (warranty, service) | Generate customer questions; write support-ready answers |
| Partnership Announcement | `biz` (partner details, integration), `stakeholder` (execs) | `product` (joint value prop) | Generate joint narrative; create technical integration summary |
| Financial Projections | `biz` (market size, pricing), `hw` (BOM, tooling) | `stakeholder` (CFO, finance) | Generate revenue ramp curves; create sensitivity scenarios |

---

## 8. Implementation & Rollout Notes

### 8.1. Database Schema Alignment

The template system maps directly to the `DocumentTemplate` and `TemplateSection` tables defined in the original PRDGenerator design:

- `DocumentTemplate` stores: `template_id`, `name`, `description`, `document_type`, `modules[]`, `defaultMetadata`
- `TemplateSection` stores: `title`, `type`, `defaultContent`, `required`, `children[]`, `prompts[]`, `dataSource`

Each template in this spec should be registered as a `DocumentTemplate` row with `template_json` containing the full section hierarchy.

### 8.2. Generation Pipeline Order

The system should generate documents in four phases to respect data dependencies:

**Phase 1: Discovery-Derived (from stakeholder conversations)**
1. Press Release
2. Product Positioning Document
3. Stakeholder Aspiration Summary
4. Internal FAQ

**Phase 2: Business-Derived (from Phase 1 + financial/operational inputs)**
5. Pricing Strategy
6. Margin Analysis
7. Revenue Projections (Financial Projections)
8. Go-to-Market Plan

**Phase 3: Execution-Derived (from Phase 1–2 + operational inputs)**
9. Sales Channel Strategy
10. Retailer Presentation Materials
11. Distribution Partner Briefs
12. Workback Schedule
13. External FAQ

**Phase 4: External-Facing (from all above)**
14. Partnership Announcements
15. Influencer/Reviewer Briefs
16. Launch Event Concepts

### 8.3. MVP Priority Recommendation

For st8 PRD system MVP, implement templates in this order:

| Tier | Templates | Rationale |
|------|-----------|-----------|
| **Tier 1: Core Reality** | Press Release, Product Positioning, Internal FAQ, Stakeholder Aspiration Summary | Generated almost entirely from stakeholder conversations; create the "reality fabric" with least input |
| **Tier 2: Business Viability** | Go-to-Market Plan, Pricing Strategy, Margin Analysis, Financial Projections | Require additional financial/operational inputs; validate business model |
| **Tier 3: Execution Readiness** | Sales Channel Strategy, Product Spec (Consumer), BOM & Cost Analysis, Certification Tracker | Needed when channels/components are identified |
| **Tier 4: Technical Depth** | Component PRD, API Specification, Architecture Overview, Test Strategy, IoT/Embedded Spec | Require deep project analysis; highest implementation complexity |
| **Tier 5: External Amplification** | Partnership Announcement, External FAQ, Messaging Framework | Generated situationally when partners or public comms are needed |

### 8.4. Quality Checklist for Template Completeness

Before marking any template as "production-ready," verify:

- [ ] Section structure matches this specification exactly
- [ ] Required/Optional flags are correctly set
- [ ] Data sources are mapped to real schema cards
- [ ] Template variables are defined with namespaces
- [ ] Inheritance chain is declared (base → extension → specialization)
- [ ] Output formats are specified for each template
- [ ] Conditional rules are documented
- [ ] Consistency rules linking to other templates are defined
- [ ] AI prompts are provided for each AI-enhanced section
- [ ] Example variable substitution is tested

### 8.5. Open Questions for Implementation

1. **Template versioning:** How do template updates affect previously generated documents? Should we auto-flag documents that used an outdated template?
2. **Custom templates:** Should users be able to create custom templates from existing documents (the "Save as Template" feature)? If so, how do we validate custom template structure against `st8-Core`?
3. **Real-time collaboration:** If multiple stakeholders edit the same template instance simultaneously, how do we resolve conflicts in variable values?
4. **Localization:** How do templates adapt to non-English markets? Should `biz.launch_region` trigger language-specific section defaults?
5. **Voice-first generation:** How do we map conversational stakeholder inputs directly to template variables without form-based UI?

---

*End of D3: Template Architecture Specification.*
